expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: scenario drafting distinguishes dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the draft explicitly separates: (a) entry paths: “Dashboard — Google Sheets export entry paths” with scenarios for “dashboard-level Export menu” and “toolbar / overflow (⋯) menu”; (b) option combinations: “Dashboard — Google Sheets export option combinations” covering “default options”, “single option toggled”, and “multiple options changed”; and (c) visible outcomes: “Dashboard — Visible completion outcomes (success / failure / cancellation)” with scenarios for success (including “open sheet link”), cancel, and failure states.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The output is titled “Phase 4a — Scenario Draft” and is structured subcategory-first (e.g., “Dashboard — Google Sheets export entry paths”, “option combinations”, “visible completion outcomes”), with nested atomic steps and verification leaves as observable outcomes (e.g., “A visible completion outcome is shown”, “A visible error state is shown”). Execution notes also claim Phase 4a contract compliance: “No canonical top-layer categories… steps are atomic and nested… Verification leaves are observable and separate from action steps.”
